figmaNodeSource: Dojo Customer App (confirmed). DS Core node ID: TODO.

variants.name: default
variants.description: Single confirmed layout: question label above two equal-width pill buttons. No additional visual variants confirmed.

sizes.name: default
sizes.description: Single confirmed size. Both option buttons are always 44px tall.

states.name: option-a-selected
states.description: The left (first) option is selected. It renders with Default button style: dark filled background, light text. The right option renders with Secondary button style: transparent background, dark text.

states.name: option-b-selected
states.description: The right (second) option is selected. It renders with Default button style: dark filled background, light text. The left option renders with Secondary button style: transparent background, dark text.

anatomyParts.name: QuestionLabel
anatomyParts.description: Text question displayed above the option group. Manrope Medium, 14px, --text/body color.

anatomyParts.name: OptionGroup
anatomyParts.description: Horizontal row containing two equal-width option buttons with 4px gap. Full-width within its container.

anatomyParts.name: OptionA
anatomyParts.description: Left option button. flex: 1, min-width 120px, height 44px, rounded-full. Renders as selected (filled) or unselected (transparent) depending on current value.

anatomyParts.name: OptionB
anatomyParts.description: Right option button. Same dimensions and behaviour as Option A.

baseComponents.name: Button — Default type
baseComponents.description: Token set used by the selected option button (filled dark background, light text).

baseComponents.name: Button — Secondary type
baseComponents.description: Token set used by the unselected option button (transparent background, dark text).

props.name: question
props.description: The question label displayed above the option buttons. Write as a full question.

props.name: options
props.description: Exactly two options. Each has a label (display text) and a value (data value).

props.name: selectedValue
props.description: The value of the currently selected option. Must always be set — no empty/unselected state.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Callback fired when the user taps an option. Receives the selected value.

notes: One option must always be selected — there is no empty state.
The component always contains exactly two options.
The selected option uses the same visual tokens as Button Default type.
The unselected option uses the same visual tokens as Button Secondary type.
Confirmed usage: Customer App 'New Plan' form — 'Do you already have accommodation?' Yes / No.
